reviewer.evidencePolicy: Only narrow deterministic claims with a checked-in evidence bundle are marked satisfied; skill-orchestration, host-semantic, and future implementation-slice claims are reclassified instead of overclaimed.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by the remaining deterministic evidence bundle: dogfood audit tests show the deterministic packet and skill control-flow boundaries are stable before broader LLM-backed cluster review.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep the review prepare, reviewer launch, and review-to-eval audit tests aligned with this claim when changing the skill or review packet workflow.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: This names bundled-skill curation behavior before HITL, not merely deterministic packet shape. It needs a dev/skill eval or audit-backed skill-flow fixture.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Author a dev/skill scenario proving that the bundled skill curates canonical specdown indexes before continuing HITL when raw candidates are too granular.
claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim is about agent skill sequencing and judgment over raw candidates, so deterministic helper tests are necessary but not sufficient.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by evaluation proof tests that distinguish fixture-backed smoke, coding-agent messaging, in-process product runner, and live/server product runner proof classes.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep evaluation proof tests updated when runner proof classes change.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by runner readiness branch-shape tests and schema fixture validation; operators get scaffolded packet actions instead of prose-only assessment authoring.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep runner readiness action packets and example assessment schema validation in sync.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by the runner assessment schema, fixture example schema tests, and runner readiness tests that durable truth lands in a runner assessment packet.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep assessment packet schema and examples versioned with runner verification contract changes.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by the skill disclosure lint: the bundled skill uses binary command discovery and avoids duplicating the command catalog.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep skill disclosure lint required and forbidden fragments aligned with command discovery changes.

unresolvedQuestions: Which host-owned adapters have actually demonstrated same-boundary external substitution?
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: The deterministic schema can require an external substitution field, but whether a substitute sits at the same boundary the product uses is host semantic judgment.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep this as a host-alignment claim until a concrete adapter assessment demonstrates same-boundary external substitution honestly.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by evaluation proof and runner readiness tests: smoke and declared proof classes can support setup confidence without being promoted to app product-path proof.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep product-path proof promotion explicit in evaluation proof tests.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by CLI smoke, runtime, and schema tests that scenario proposal packets preserve the full ranked proposal list while deriving a bounded attention view.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep full proposal list preservation tested when changing scenario proposal output.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by the canonical claim map test that scenario history, scenario proposal source, and normalizer raw claims are absorbed into one maintainer-facing claim while preserving fingerprints.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep canonical-map absorption tests updated with scenario-history and scenario-proposal contract renames.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by optimize CLI smoke and agent-runtime tests that build-artifact emits one durable revision packet reopenable without rediscovering optimize inputs by hand.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep optimize build-artifact smoke and revision artifact schema tests aligned.

unresolvedQuestions: Should this implementation-slice sentence be removed, rewritten as shipped behavior, or left as historical planning text?
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: This is todo-style implementation-slice wording, not a stable shipped behavior claim to satisfy directly.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Do not satisfy directly; rewrite or move this sentence out of the active contract claim surface if it should not remain a proof obligation.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by optimize-flow tests that generated optimize proposals include one revision brief, bounded stop conditions, and no compile loop, iteration plan, or target patch.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep optimize proposal tests strict about bounded revision semantics.
